context.story_engine: A high school chemistry teacher diagnosed with inoperable lung cancer turns to manufacturing and selling methamphetamine in order to secure his family's future.

plotlines.name: Walt: Empire
plotlines.goal: build a methamphetamine business to secure his family's financial future
plotlines.obstacle: violent dealers, lack of distribution network, escalating danger

plotlines.name: Hank: Investigation
plotlines.goal: find the source of the high-purity blue methamphetamine
plotlines.obstacle: no direct leads, sophisticated chemistry suggests educated cook

plotlines.name: Jesse: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive as Walt's partner in the drug business
plotlines.obstacle: violent dealers, lack of street credibility, Walt's dangerous decisions

plotlines.name: Walt: Treatment
plotlines.goal: deal with his lung cancer diagnosis and treatment
plotlines.obstacle: family pressure to fight, financial burden, physical deterioration

events.event: Walter White works at a car wash to support his pregnant wife and disabled son
At Walt's 50th birthday party, Hank shows off his gun and invites Walt on a DEA ride-along
During the meth lab raid, Walt spots his former student Jesse escaping
Walt collapses at the car wash and is rushed to the hospital
Doctor diagnoses Walt with inoperable Stage III lung cancer, two years to live
Walt tells no one about the diagnosis
Walt drives to Jesse's house and proposes a meth cooking partnership
Jesse laughs at the idea but agrees after Walt threatens to turn him in to Hank
Jesse purchases an old RV to serve as a mobile lab
Walt and Jesse drive to the desert to cook their first batch
Walt's chemistry produces 99.1 percent pure methamphetamine
Krazy-8 and Emilio arrive at the cook site, having followed Jesse
Emilio recognizes Walt from the DEA ride-along and accuses Jesse of working with police
Krazy-8 and Emilio force Walt and Jesse into the RV at gunpoint
Walt deliberately creates phosphine gas and escapes the RV, jamming the door
Emilio dies from the gas, Krazy-8 collapses unconscious
Walt drives the RV away with both bodies inside, crashing it into a ditch
Walt records a video confession and farewell message to his family
Walt raises a gun toward approaching vehicles, which turn out to be fire trucks

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore how facing death transforms Walt's moral boundaries
Walt and Jesse's fates become intertwined when they face Krazy-8 and Emilio together
the audience knows Walt's cancer diagnosis drives his actions, but his family doesn't

events.event: Walt and Jesse return from the desert with the RV containing Emilio's body and an unconscious Krazy-8
Jesse is panicked and furious, blaming Walt for the catastrophe
Walt takes charge and assigns tasks: Jesse must dispose of Emilio's body, Walt will deal with Krazy-8
They lock Krazy-8 in Jesse's basement using a bicycle lock around a pole
Walt goes home and lies next to Skyler, hiding all evidence of the night
Skyler notices Walt's strange behavior and presses him, but he deflects
Walt brings food and water down to Krazy-8 and they have cautious conversations
Krazy-8 tells Walt about his family's furniture business and how he fell into dealing
Walt begins to feel sympathy and considers releasing Krazy-8
Jesse dissolves Emilio's body in hydrofluoric acid inside a regular household bathtub, ignoring Walt's instructions
The acid eats through the tub, floor, and ceiling, sending dissolved remains crashing through the hallway
Walt arrives at Jesse's house to find the destruction and is horrified
Walt makes Jesse clean up the mess
Walt reconstructs a broken plate from the trash and discovers a large shard is missing
Walt realizes Krazy-8 has concealed the plate shard as a weapon
Krazy-8 lunges at Walt with the plate shard as Walt unlocks the bicycle lock
Walt pulls the lock tight around Krazy-8's throat and strangles him to death
Walt sits on the floor weeping after killing Krazy-8
Walt and Jesse bury the bodies in the desert
Skyler grows more suspicious of Walt's absences
Walt deflects Skyler's suspicions by claiming he is taking long walks to process stress
Hank mentions at family dinner that the DEA is investigating a new high-purity meth

interactions.description: both Walt and Jesse face life-or-death decisions that force them to cross moral lines
Hank discusses investigating the high-purity meth that Walt is producing, unaware his brother-in-law is the cook
Walt's criminal actions force him deeper into lies with his family

events.event: Walt finally tells Skyler about his lung cancer diagnosis
Skyler is devastated and immediately begins researching treatment options, oncologists, and costs
She insists they fight it aggressively
Walt resists, saying the treatment will bankrupt the family and only buy a few months
Skyler schedules an appointment with an oncologist anyway and tells her sister Marie Schrader, who is shocked
Hank tries to offer Walt support in his gruff way, but Walt shuts down any emotional conversation
The family gathers for an uncomfortable intervention-style meeting where Skyler, Hank, Marie, and Walter Jr. each share their feelings about the diagnosis and urge Walt to pursue treatment
Walter Jr. passionately argues that his father must fight
Walt listens quietly and ultimately agrees to undergo chemotherapy — not because he wants to, but because the family pressure leaves him no choice
Jesse tries to sell the remaining meth from their first cook
He approaches his regular contacts on the street but quickly discovers that without Krazy-8 as a distributor, he has no reliable network
Jesse tries selling small amounts himself at a biker bar and nearly gets robbed
He realizes he cannot distribute the product alone and needs a new connection
Walt and Jesse meet to discuss their situation: they have a superior product but no distribution channel
Jesse suggests they need to establish territory, which means dealing with other dealers
Walt is reluctant but understands the logic
At a follow-up medical appointment, Walt learns the chemotherapy will cost thousands per session even with insurance
He calculates the total financial burden on a napkin and recognizes that his teacher's salary cannot cover both treatment and his family's future security
This hardens his resolve to continue cooking
Skyler creates an online fundraising page for Walt's treatment, which humiliates him
Marie shoplifts a decorative tiara and gives it to Skyler as a baby shower gift, a habit that will later cause tension

episodes.theme: the burden of choice when family obligations conflict with personal autonomy

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters being forced into unwanted situations by circumstances beyond their control
the cost of chemotherapy directly motivates Walt's decision to continue cooking meth
Jesse's distribution problems become Walt and Jesse's shared business challenge

events.event: Walt begins chemotherapy, which leaves him exhausted and nauseous
Walt hides the severity of the side effects from Skyler, vomiting in private
Skyler notices Walt's deterioration and grows increasingly protective
Walt finds Skyler's protectiveness suffocating
Walt snaps at a student at school
Walt stares at his reflection as clumps of hair fall out
Walt decides to shave his head completely
Walter Jr. shaves his own head in solidarity with Walt
Hank and Gomez investigate the new blue-tinted methamphetamine in Albuquerque
DEA traces the supply back to a low-level dealer
DEA learns the product has unusually high purity, suggesting a skilled chemist
Jesse reconnects with Badger and recruits him to sell on the street
Jesse pushes into territory near the crossroads
A rival dealer confronts Jesse in a standoff that Jesse wins by bluffing
Skyler discovers the baby shower tiara Marie gave her was shoplifted
Skyler confronts Marie, who denies shoplifting the tiara
The sisters' relationship fractures as Skyler refuses to speak to Marie
Walt and Jesse cook a second batch in the RV in the desert
Walt insists on maintaining high purity standards
Jesse starts selling through Badger but the amounts are small
Walt calculates they need larger quantities and tells Jesse they need a real distributor

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore different forms of personal transformation and the price paid for change
Hank investigates the high-purity blue meth without knowing Walt is the chemist he's looking for
Walt's physical deterioration from cancer parallels his moral deterioration from cooking meth

events.event: Skyler organizes a family gathering and invites Ted Beneke
Walt becomes uncomfortable and jealous of Ted's presence
Walt overcompensates by drinking too much
Walt forces Walter Jr. to drink tequila until he vomits
Hank confronts Walt and takes the bottle away
Skyler demands to know what is wrong with Walt beyond the cancer
Walt offers no explanation to Skyler
Jesse learns about Tuco Salamanca as a mid-level distributor
Jesse approaches Skinny Pete for an introduction to Tuco
Skinny Pete warns Jesse that Tuco is dangerously unpredictable
Skinny Pete arranges a meeting with Tuco
Jesse goes to Tuco's headquarters and presents the blue meth sample
Tuco tests the meth and is impressed by the quality
Jesse proposes a distribution deal with Tuco
Tuco agrees to buy but then beats Jesse severely and takes the meth without paying
Jesse calls Walt from the hospital, battered and humiliated
Walt visits Jesse in the hospital and sees his injuries
Hank and Gomez process the beating as a drug-related assault
Jesse refuses to cooperate with the police
Walt realizes they cannot be victims in this world
Walt resolves to confront Tuco himself
Walt takes the remaining meth and prepares for a direct meeting with Tuco

episodes.theme: the cost of pride and the necessity of respect in dangerous worlds

interactions.description: both plotlines explore Walt's struggle with inadequacy and his need to assert dominance
Jesse's beating forces Walt to take direct action, merging their fates
Hank processes Jesse's beating as routine drug violence, unaware of Walt's involvement

events.event: Walt synthesizes mercury fulminate explosive disguised as meth
Walt drives to Tuco's headquarters alone
Tuco's guards let Walt inside, amused by his appearance
Walt introduces himself as Heisenberg and demands payment plus future deal
Tuco laughs and threatens Walt
Walt throws mercury fulminate crystal, causing explosion
Tuco agrees to pay for stolen meth and buy future batches for fifty thousand
Walt returns home with cash, buzzing with adrenaline
Walt has aggressive, spontaneous sex with Skyler
DEA identifies blue meth as extremely pure with advanced chemistry signature
Hank presents findings suggesting cook has advanced chemistry training
DEA investigation focuses on known drug operations but finds no match
Skyler notices Walt carrying extra cash and behaving erratically
Skyler asks pointed questions about Walt's whereabouts
Walt fabricates story about winning money at poker with colleagues
Skyler is skeptical but lets it go for now
Jesse learns Walt made a deal with Tuco
Jesse is relieved but unnerved by Walt's transformation
Walt and Jesse agree to cook Tuco's order immediately

episodes.theme: the intoxicating power of fear and respect

interactions.description: Walt's newfound power from confronting Tuco manifests as sexual aggression at home, showing how criminal confidence bleeds into personal life
Hank correctly deduces the cook has advanced chemistry training while unknowingly describing his brother-in-law
Walt's deal with Tuco directly affects Jesse's safety and their partnership dynamic

events.event: Walt and Jesse cook Tuco's two-pound order in the RV, working through the night to meet the deadline
Walt is meticulous and refuses to cut corners despite Jesse's urge to rush
The cook yields their purest batch yet
They arrange the delivery with Tuco at his headquarters
Tuco tests the product, declares it excellent, and pays the fifty thousand dollars as agreed
Tuco immediately demands double the quantity for the next delivery, on a tighter schedule
Walt hesitates but agrees, recognizing that refusing Tuco is not a realistic option
Skyler discovers a second cell phone hidden in Walt's belongings
When she confronts him, Walt claims it was a replacement phone he bought at the school but forgot about
Skyler does not believe him and begins quietly investigating on her own, checking phone records and tracking his absences
Hank celebrates a promotion within the DEA's Albuquerque office
At a family dinner, Hank describes the blue meth case to the table, explaining that the product has disrupted the local drug economy
Walt listens intently, masking his reaction, and asks casual questions about the investigation
Walt processes what he has learned: the DEA is actively hunting the source of his product, and his own brother-in-law is leading the effort
Jesse tells Walt they should find another buyer, but Walt argues that Tuco's volume and cash make him irreplaceable
During their next delivery, Tuco beats one of his own associates nearly to death over a minor perceived slight
Walt and Jesse stand frozen, unable to intervene
As they leave, both understand they are now trapped in a business relationship with a man who could kill them on impulse
Walt drives home in silence, parks in his driveway, and sits staring at his house — the family inside unaware of the danger he has brought into their lives

episodes.theme: the price of success in a world without rules

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore the escalating costs of Walt's choices — business success brings danger, survival requires deeper deception
the audience knows Walt is the target of Hank's investigation while the family celebrates Hank's promotion
Walt's business success with Tuco directly creates the survival threat for both him and Jesse
